name: Snow Report 2026-03-12 16:21:34
report:
Good evening, Beast family,

The weather overnight made for some challenging conditions today, but we made it happen thanks to our patrol and operations teams. We’ve got good news as the storm coming in tomorrow night has been bumped up to a Winter Weather Advisory with 5 to 9 inches forecasted once it’s all said and done. Mother nature isn’t done bringing winter just quit yet and that extra base will be excellent for later in the season. 

Tomorrow brings a mix of sun and clouds with a high near 29 at the base and 22 at the top. Winds shift to the south through the afternoon, and snow is likely to move in late in the day with the potential for 1 to 2 inches of fresh.

Surface conditions will continue to be variable, consisting of loose granular, machine groomed, and scattered areas of hardpack and thin cover, although we still have plenty of snow on our base. Groomers will again be the best choice for making turns. Our grooming team will be getting to Outer Limits, Cruise Control, Bunny Buster, and many more. Check out the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown on our terrain.

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. We're not done making snow quite yet as the crew will be firing back up next week. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off (spoiler alert: they’re paying off big time).

Scheduled lifts will spin at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad opening at 9:30 a.m. The South Ridge and Canyon Quads will be spinning at 10:00 a.m. 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. The Stash is also open again after last weekend’s Slash & Berm Banked Slalom and is back in play. Just as a heads up, Dream Maker, Easy Street, and Lil' Stash parks will not open today due to early morning re-freezing. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

Grab your 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Today is your last day to unlock all the best of springtime at The Beast for $379! Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass at any time for just $199. 

Tomorrow, Rentals & demos for the weekend can be picked up ahead of time between 2:00 p.m. and 6:00 p.m. 

Finally, the Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill is bringing a one-night-only Jamaica Carnival–inspired dinner to the Clubhouse Grill. Pre-order here by 12:00 p.m. on Saturday, March 14 and pick up your meal between 6:00 and 7:30 p.m. after a day on the mountain. Quantities are limited and all orders must be prepaid online with no substitutions, so be sure to order early.

Let’s get after it!

 – Evan

 

Operating Day: 122

Lifts       

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.      

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.       

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!       

The Tubing Park presented by bubly remains open Friday - Sunday through March 22.  

Uphill Travel       

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.    

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-12 06:24:59
report:
Good Morning, Beast Fam,

After the showers moved through, we had another beauty day of shredding spring slopes under the sun yesterday, and the party keeps rolling as we're still leading the East with our trail offerings.

Overnight we had scattered showers, and this morning brings a slight chance of rain and snow showers early before tapering off. Expect cloudy skies, falling temperatures through the day, and a stout west wind that will keep things feeling brisk. Gusts could reach up to 45 mph, and while snowfall should remain minimal, a light dusting is possible.

Snow surfaces were cutting beautifully like soft butter yesterday and gave that feeling of confidence to lean further into your turns. The snow continues to soften as we move deeper into spring skiing, and even with the precipitation overnight, surfaces should remain soft and fun as things likely clear up around opening. Skyelark was a joy to shred under the sun yesterday and should be a great one to add back to your list today. Head over to Bear Claw and Mouse Run for more great skiing and riding.

Our grooming team worked on terrain parks in The Stash and Timberline along with more than fifty other trails receiving a refresh. Among them is Frolic, perfect for those after easy cruising, and Skyeburst, which offers a bit more speed to carve. Full House is always a great place to get the legs warmed up before stepping up to carving. Check out the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown on our terrain.

If groomers aren’t your style, get your favorite natural snow runs in while you can. Even with the recent warm-up, we still have well over 110 trails and 12 lifts to choose from. The Canyon Quad is scheduled to offer lift access starting at 10:00 a.m. If your legs get tired from navigating the softer snow, there’s always a tasty lodge beverage waiting for you.

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us for now, but we're also not done making snow quite yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off (spoiler alert: they’re paying off big time).

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. The Stash is also open again after last weekend’s Slash & Berm Banked Slalom and is back in play. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

Today is your last chance to snag your 2026 Spring Pass at the best price, offering unlimited skiing and riding from March 13 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass any time for just $199. Beginning March 13, the Spring Pass will jump up in price.

The spring skiing continues!

-Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-11 05:50:42
report:
Good morning, Beast fans! 

Yesterday was a textbook spring ripper on the hill. Comfortable temps, soft snow, and blue skies made it the kind of day you didn’t want to end. The K-1 patio was the place to be by the afternoon, and I can’t blame anyone who made that decision. Today will bring the same friendly temperatures but with some more cloud coverage. 

Looking at the weather, it seems like we’re going to dodge most of the unfriendly precipitation during the day. Southward winds will be a bit stronger than yesterday at higher altitudes, but not nearly enough to make it feel very cold at all. As of now, we’re due for a slight inversion, as temperatures both at the base and summit will float around 45-50°F all day. 

As for the snow surfaces, if you prefer a softer snow with some grip, then you’ll be in the right place. Conditions were dynamite all throughout yesterday afternoon. Cascade (top to bottom) was my vote for trail of the day, and it should still be nice today—not too bumpy, easy turns to be found everywhere, and no patchiness in snow coverage. East Fall was another option with a few more bumps but no bad turns at all. Needles Eye got a little bit slower by the afternoon after sitting in the sun, but you won’t catch me complaining about classic spring weather. 

Our groomers tackled 67 trails last night, with a few trails coming off the plan to preserve snow during the warmer temps overnight. High Traverse will not have corduroy but will still be just fine for traversing today, while Cascade will also be left alone. Classic groomers like Cruise Control and Bunny Buster will have wide swathes of corduroy. Check out the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates there.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-10 16:08:18
report:
Good evening, Beast fans! 

Today was a textbook spring day on the hill. Comfortable temps, soft snow, and blue skies made it the kind of day you didn’t want to end. The K-1 patio was the place to be by the afternoon, and I can’t blame anyone who made that decision. Tomorrow will bring the same friendly temperatures but with some more cloud coverage. 

Looking at the weather, it seems like we’re going to dodge most of the unfriendly precipitation during the day tomorrow. There’s a chance of getting a bit overnight, but the skies should mostly hold up tomorrow before lifts close. Southward winds will be a bit stronger than today at higher altitudes, but not nearly enough to make it feel very cold at all. As of now, we’re due for a slight inversion tomorrow, as temperatures both at the base and summit will float around 45-50°F all day. 

As for the snow surfaces, if you prefer a softer snow with some grip, then you’ll be in the right place tomorrow. Conditions were dynamite all throughout this afternoon. Cascade (top to bottom) was my vote for trail of the day, and it should still be nice tomorrow—not too bumpy, easy turns to be found everywhere, and no patchiness in snow coverage. East Fall was another option with a few more bumps but no bad turns at all. Needles Eye got a little bit slower by the afternoon after sitting in the sun, but you won’t catch me complaining about classic spring weather. 

Our groomers will set out to tackle 67 trails tonight, with a few trails coming off the plan to preserve snow during the warmer temps we’ll continue to see through the night. High Traverse will not have corduroy but will still be just fine for traversing tomorrow, while Cascade will also be left alone for tonight. Classic groomers like Cruise Control and Bunny Buster will have wide swathes of corduory by the morning. Check out the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates there. 

If groomers aren’t your style, get your favorite natural snow runs in while you can. Even with this recent warm-up, we’re still heading into tomorrow with 140 trails and 15 lifts to choose from. The South Ridge and Canyon Quads are lined up to offer bonus midweek lift access tomorrow starting at 10:00 a.m. If your legs get tired from navigating the softer snow, there’s always a tasty lodge beverage waiting for you. 

Our snowmaking team took advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us for now, but we're also not done making snow quite yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off (spoiler alert: they’re paying off big time). 

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. The Stash is also open again after last weekend’s Slash & Berm Banked Slalom and is back in play. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details. 

Finally, the 2026 Spring Pass is still on sale now, offering unlimited skiing & riding from March 13 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade to a Spring Pass any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to lock in the best price at $379. 

We’ll see you out there again tomorrow! 

- Cullen

It definitely felt like spring out there yesterday and today should be much of the same. Snow surfaces will likely again follow a classic spring cycle today. Conditions should start off firmer this morning before softening nicely as the sun works its magic. By the afternoon, many areas of the mountain should be skiing soft and forgiving with a few slower spots here and there as temperatures climb. Even with the seasonal transition underway, we still have plenty of coverage across the mountain and continue to lead the East with the most open terrain available anywhere in the region.

Expect plenty of sunshine again today, with temperatures pushing toward 50 degrees up top and into the mid-50s at the base. Consider putting on some sunscreen today if you plan to be out on the hill for a little while! Winds will come out of the west-southwest around 15 to 25 mph with some higher gusts, but overall it should feel like another great spring ski day.

Morning groomers will again be the move. Our team resurfaced more than 75 trails overnight, setting up smooth corduroy for early laps before the afternoon softening kicks in. Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. name: Snow Report 2026-03-09 16:11:33
report:
Good evening, Beast family,

It definitely felt like spring out there today. Sunshine returned in full force and the snow softened quickly under the warm temperatures, making for another fun day of cruising, carving, and soaking up the sun across the mountain. The breeze kept things moving along, but overall it was a beautiful day to be out making turns.

Snow surfaces followed a classic spring cycle. Conditions started off firmer this morning before softening nicely as the sun worked its magic. By the afternoon, many areas were skiing soft and forgiving with a few slower spots here and there as temperatures climbed. Even with the seasonal transition underway, we still have plenty of coverage across the mountain and continue to lead the East with the most open terrain available anywhere in the region.

Tomorrow is shaping up to be a near clone of today. Expect plenty of sunshine again, with temperatures pushing toward 50 degrees up top and the upper 50s at the base. Winds will come out of the west-southwest around 15 to 25 mph with some higher gusts, but overall it should feel like another great spring ski day.

Morning groomers will again be the move. Our team will be resurfacing more than 75 trails overnight, setting up smooth corduroy for early laps before the afternoon softening kicks in. Runs like Bunny Buster, Header, Needles Eye, and Skyeburst should be great places to start the day. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report first thing for all the latest updates.

Our snowmaking team took full advantage of the most recent cold stretch last week, firing on the Superstar Glacier from top to bottom and building base depths in key areas around the mountain. Temps have risen out of range, and our snowmaking team has brought the system offline. We've got some good spring skiing ahead of us over the next couple of weeks, but we're not done making snow yet. Check out our latest Insider Blog to see how recent snowmaking investments have been paying off.

In our kparks, Ramshead continues to be the place for development and progression, with Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard offering fun XS and S features. Timberline remains stacked with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Over at Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including that sweet rail section near the base. Check the Terrain Parks Report for full details.

The Stash reopened today after the weekend’s Slash & Berm Banked Slalom and is back in play. Lil’ Stash remains closed as our team continues refreshing that zone.

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale now for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13 through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. March 12 is the last day to lock in the best price at $379.

Enjoy these beautiful days while we have them. Around here we sometimes call this “false spring,” and there are hints that some more winter-like weather could return later in the week and into the weekend. We’ll see you out there tomorrow!

 – Josh
